llm_summary: The Spotify Privacy Policy now specifies that it applies to the personal data of U.S. residents, rather than just stating it applies to users in general. It clarifies that certain privacy rights are provided to all U.S. residents, not just those in specific states. Additionally, the policy has removed references to specific types of data, such as protected classifications, internet activity, and geolocation data.

modified_paragraphs.after: This Privacy Policy describes how we at Spotify USA Inc. process personal data of U.S. residents. From now on, we’ll call it the ‘Policy’.
modified_paragraphs.before: This Privacy Policy describes how we process your personal data at Spotify USA Inc. From now on, we’ll call it the ‘Policy’.

modified_paragraphs.after: From time to time, we may develop new or offer additional services. They’ll also be subject to this Policy, unless stated otherwise when we introduce them. Our California Notice at Collection as required by the California Consumer Privacy Act applies to residents of California. If you are a resident of California, you should read this notice together with this Policy.
modified_paragraphs.before: From time to time, we may develop new or offer additional services. They’ll also be subject to this Policy, unless stated otherwise when we introduce them. IIf you reside in California, our supplemental California Notice at Collection, applies in addition to this Policy.

modified_paragraphs.after: Certain U.S. states have adopted privacy laws that give certain rights to individuals over their personal data. We provide these rights to all residents of the U.S., regardless of where they live.
modified_paragraphs.before: Privacy laws give certain rights to individuals over their personal data.

removed_paragraphs: Characteristics of protected classifications under California or U.S. law (i.e., your age and gender)
Internet or other electronic network activity information;
Geolocation data (i.e.,your non-precise location as derived from your IP address);
